metadata.notes: Vibe is an interactive Textual TUI/REPL, but vibe/agent.go declares no Control -> daemon offers no backchannel control today; feasible adapter fix (declare Control{SupportsInput:true,Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC} like kirocli).

assessment.body:
## Verdict: agent=yes, daemon=bug, driver=ready

**agent=yes.** Mistral Vibe (v2.19.0) is an interactive Textual TUI/REPL: `VibeApp(App)` in `vibe/cli/textual_ui/app.py` reads typed input through a `Prompt` widget, dispatches `SlashCommand`/`UserCommandMessage`, and buffers turns via `MessageQueue` (message_queue.py) with a `terminal_input_filter.py`. Architecturally it therefore CAN be driven by injected keystrokes delivered into its controlling terminal (tmux/kitty/iTerm2/Terminal.app), exactly the backchannel-control mechanism, plus slash presets like /compact.

**daemon=bug.** The vibe adapter declares NO `Control` (the field is left at its zero value) in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/agent.go`. Zero-value Control means `SupportsInput=false`, no `Interrupt`, no `Presets` -> the daemon derives the session as non-controllable and will NOT offer backchannel input/interrupt/preset for vibe. The adapter comment states this was a DELIBERATE conservative choice ("Vibe's backchannel (interactive input injection) is unverified, so the adapter is read-only until a live drive confirms it"). This is a feasible adapter change, not an architectural limit: declare `Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:agent.InterruptCtrlC, Presets:{...}}` and add `agent.ControlPermission()` to `Permissions`, exactly as the sibling kirocli adapter does (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go:44` -> `Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC}` + `agent.ControlPermission()`). Because vibe's REPL accepts typed input the behavior is observable once declared -> `bug` (product; file an issue and record known_failing), not `incapable`. Once Control is declared the shared control seam (InputService gate: backchannel toggle -> control consent -> controllable; Controller tmux/kitty injectors keyed off Launcher) applies unchanged, and the verify contract for the new `modify`-kind ControlPermission is `contracttesting.AssertPermissionGated` (core/internal/contracttesting/permission_gate.go).

**driver=ready.** `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` implements every step the recipe needs — `send`, `slash`, `sleep`, `interrupt` (plus `keys`) are all present. DRIVE_ELICITS (send slash sleep) are all covered; no `gap:*`.

Route: record-known-failing — record against the correct spec (session advances ready->working on injected input, settles to ready) and file the adapter-Control issue; the recording fails until vibe/agent.go declares Control.
caveats: Emission unverified: that injected keystrokes actually drive vibe's Textual TUI to a new turn (and Ctrl-C interrupts it) has not been confirmed by a live drive — the adapter itself left backchannel 'unverified'. Confidence held at 0.8 until record proves the terminal-injection path; record may promote daemon=bug into a concrete filed issue or, if injection does not take, reconsider.
Vibe default model not pinned in the spec observations (not confirmed from the live surface); record fills it from the recording.

sources.note: vibe adapter declares NO Control (zero value) -> SupportsInput=false; comment states backchannel deliberately unverified/read-only
reference fix: sibling declares Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC} + agent.ControlPermission() (line 44)
Control{SupportsInput,Interrupt,Presets} axis, InterruptCtrlC, and ControlPermission() helper (KindModify)
AssertPermissionGated — verify contract for the ControlPermission a Control declaration would add
VibeApp(App) Textual TUI/REPL: Prompt widget, SlashCommand/UserCommandMessage dispatch -> reads typed terminal input (agent=yes)
MessageQueue turn buffering + terminal_input_filter.py — confirms interactive input surface

script.text: Write a haiku about a lighthouse, then wait for my next message.
Now write a second haiku about the sea.
